req.title: Savings goal (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall create a savings goal with a target amount and target date; progress shall be computed nightly. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.
preconditions: authenticated

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-046 mandates goal creation with target_amount and target_date, nightly progress initialisation, 422 on target <= 0, and explicit boundary coverage. Four tests are designed: (1) EP positive – valid mid-range amount creates goal with progress=0; (2) NEG negative – target_amount=0 returns 422; (3) BVA boundary – exact minimum valid amount (0.01) creates goal; (4) BVA boundary – exact maximum allowed amount creates goal. Together these satisfy EP, NEG, and BVA heuristics and cover the happy path, error pathway, and both boundary thresholds cited in the requirement.

tests.name: test_create_savings_goal_valid_amount
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
banking-api /goals endpoint is reachable
Target date is a future ISO-8601 date
actions: POST /goals with target_amount=500.00 and a future target_date
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a non-empty goal_id
Response body contains progress field equal to 0

tests.name: test_create_savings_goal_zero_amount_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
banking-api /goals endpoint is reachable
Target date is a future ISO-8601 date
actions: POST /goals with target_amount=0 and a future target_date
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error message referencing target_amount
tests.executable:
def test_create_savings_goal_zero_amount_returns_422(auth_token, api_client, future_date):
    payload = {
        "target_amount": 0,
        "target_date": future_date
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/goals", json=payload, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"})
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "target_amount" in error_text or "target" in error_text, \
        f"Error body should reference target_amount: {body}"

tests.name: test_create_savings_goal_negative_amount_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
banking-api /goals endpoint is reachable
Target date is a future ISO-8601 date
actions: POST /goals with target_amount=-1 and a future target_date
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error message referencing target_amount
tests.executable:
def test_create_savings_goal_negative_amount_returns_422(auth_token, api_client, future_date):
    payload = {
        "target_amount": -1,
        "target_date": future_date
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/goals", json=payload, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"})
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "target_amount" in error_text or "target" in error_text, \
        f"Error body should reference target_amount: {body}"

tests.name: test_create_savings_goal_minimum_valid_amount
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
banking-api /goals endpoint is reachable
Target date is a future ISO-8601 date
Minimum allowed target_amount is 0.01 (one cent)
actions: POST /goals with target_amount=0.01 (exact lower bound) and a future target_date
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a non-empty goal_id
Response body contains progress field equal to 0
trace: R-FIN-046: boundary behaviour at exact min threshold

tests.name: test_create_savings_goal_maximum_valid_amount
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
banking-api /goals endpoint is reachable
Target date is a future ISO-8601 date
Maximum allowed target_amount is defined by MAX_GOAL_AMOUNT fixture (e.g. 999999999.99)
actions: POST /goals with target_amount=MAX_GOAL_AMOUNT (exact upper bound) and a future target_date
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a non-empty goal_id
Response body contains progress field equal to 0
trace: R-FIN-046: boundary behaviour at exact max threshold

tests.name: test_create_savings_goal_state_initialised_before_nightly_run
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
banking-api /goals endpoint is reachable
Nightly progress computation has NOT yet run for today
actions: POST /goals with target_amount=1000.00 and a future target_date
Assert goal is in CREATED / ACTIVE state with progress=0
expected: progress field equals 0 (nightly job has not yet run)
trace: R-FIN-046: progress shall be computed nightly – initial state before first nightly run
tests.executable:
def test_create_savings_goal_state_initialised_before_nightly_run(auth_token, api_client, future_date):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    create_payload = {"target_amount": 1000.00, "target_date": future_date}
    create_resp = api_client.post("/goals", json=create_payload, headers=headers)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201, f"Creation failed: {create_resp.text}"
    goal_id = create_resp.json()["goal_id"]

    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/goals/{goal_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, f"GET failed: {get_resp.text}"
    body = get_resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") in ("active", "created"), \
        f"Unexpected status: {body.get('status')}"
    assert body.get("progress") == 0, \
        f"Progress before nightly run must be 0, got {body.get('progress')}"
